name: Center Flow (The Funnel)
watch_out: Water-spitting holes on upper pitches requiring careful water flow avoidance
Thin ice sections requiring half-screws and tying off placements; carry stubbers and rock gear for security
Detached or melting ice sections dependent on temperature and recent warm spells
Mixed climbing finish on pitch 2 (right-facing corner) requiring rock climbing technique
Belay anchors may be limited to slung icicles or minimal rock placements; verify before descent
North-facing aspect means ice quality extends late into season but melt-cycle sensitivity in spring
access_hazards: Bridge Creek Campground area subject to seasonal raptor (golden eagle) closure Jan 1 - Aug 15; do not approach within 1/2 mile of Bridge Creek Wall during closure
Approach via Bridge Creek Road with variable snow cover; slippery conditions underfoot
Boulder field approach through open glades with hidden boulders under light snow
seasonal_window: December through March; peaks January-February; late season viability due to NE aspect
ice_quality: Thick ice on central flow; prone to bonding issues after warm spells; freezing rain events create supportable crust layers but faceting risk below
sources: Mountain Project Ice Climbing in Icicle Creek

name: Left of Hubba Hubba (Left Flow)
watch_out: Thin smear ice on slab; protection very difficult with rock pro and pitons required
Discontinuous ice sections requiring run-out climbing on first pitch; only short but slightly exposed
Good belay on ledge but second pitch thin and protection-challenging; ice screw placements unreliable
Lower angle approach may be slower melt-out but still susceptible to rapid degradation in warm cycles
Popular beginner route so hazards often downplayed; requires competent belay and screw placement skills
access_hazards: Same approach as Center Flow; variable snow cover and slippery conditions
Raptor closure Jan 1 - Aug 15 on Bridge Creek Wall (1/2 mile buffer)
seasonal_window: December through April; excellent beginner-to-intermediate season; long viability on NE aspect
ice_quality: Thin, delicate ice on slab; forms consistently but quality varies; prefers stable cold spells without freeze-thaw cycles

name: Right of Hubba Hubba (Right Flow)
watch_out: Thin ice throughout; ice screws feel insecure; bring stubbers and rock gear for backup
Climbing easier than appearance from distance but ice quality demands caution
Single-pitch format limits escape options; if ice becomes unreliable mid-pitch, retreating becomes urgent
Less-protected route than center and left; fewer natural belay ledges
access_hazards: Located 50 feet right and up from main Hubba Hubba; approach same as other flows
Raptor closure Jan 1 - Aug 15 bridge creek wall
seasonal_window: December through March; shorter season than left/center due to thinner ice
ice_quality: Tends to be thin; forms later in season and sheds faster; sensitive to solar warming on single-pitch
sources: Mountain Project Right of Hubba Hubba

name: Icicle Buttress - Left Side
area: Icicle Creek - Icicle Buttress Ice Climbs
height: 2,090 ft total area; multiple pitch options
watch_out: Lower-angle ice on slab can be thin and brittle; ice screw placements marginal
Slab aspect increases slipping hazard if ice degrades to water-ice film
Mixed climbing sections (M2-M3) require rock gear and climbing shoes; be prepared to switch between ice and rock
Multiple pitch options mean route-finding complexity; establish clear descent plan before committing
access_hazards: Located 6.4 miles down Icicle Road from Bridge Creek Campground
Winter road closure may require snowshoe or ski approach
Avalanche terrain; observe NWAC Icicle Creek observations for slope angle and aspect stability
seasonal_window: January through February peak; shoulder season (December, early March) highly variable
ice_quality: Formation depends on sustained cold and regular meltwater flow; sensitive to warm spells that thin bonding
sources: Mountain Project Icicle Buttress Ice climbs

name: Icicle Buttress - Middle Section
area: Icicle Creek - Icicle Buttress Ice Climbs
watch_out: Steeper ice (70-80 degrees) requiring advanced ice climbing; longer, sustained sections with fewer rests
Upper pitches can reach WI4 (continuous 80-degree ice); fatigue management critical
Mixed climbing (M2-M4) on rocks between ice; requires dual skillset and gear management
Rock gear placements may be poor quality; verify anchors before committing to top-out
Exposure increases with height; falls higher on pitch have longer run-out
access_hazards: Approach 6.4 miles down Icicle Road; winter conditions may close road
Avalanche slope assessment required; slopes facing NE/N are most stable but still require judgment
Descent route-finding in darkness or whiteout complex; plan exit before ascending
seasonal_window: January and early February; upper pitches (WI4) require optimal conditions
ice_quality: Steeper terrain creates thicker, more stable ice but also freeze-thaw cycling creates dinner-plating risk in afternoons
sources: Mountain Project Icicle Buttress Ice climbs

name: Icicle Buttress - Right Side (Right Flow)
area: Icicle Creek - Icicle Buttress Ice Climbs
watch_out: Steepest section of buttress with continuous 80-90 degree ice; WI4 sustained climbing with potential WI5 crux
Mixed climbing (M2-M4) interspersed; requires full rack of rock gear and ice screws; weight management critical
Limited rest stances; high consequence climbing on upper pitches
Ice quality can deteriorate quickly; first ascent of day preferred to assess conditions
Serac/icefall hazard if climbing in direct sun; early morning only recommended
Rope management and belay placement challenging in steep terrain
access_hazards: Same approach as left/middle; road may be closed winter
Avalanche slope assessment: right side may face more solar exposure and wind slabbing
Descent likely requires rappelling; anchors may be suboptimal; have backup anchor materials
seasonal_window: January through early February only; requires optimal ice conditions
ice_quality: Steeper pitch = thicker ice but also greater freeze-thaw stress; brittle ice and dinner-plating common
sources: Mountain Project Icicle Buttress Ice climbs

name: Buttress Left (mixed climbing variant)
watch_out: Mixed climbing (M2) dominant; carry rock gear and ice screws in equal measure
First pitch steeper than many Icicle Creek routes; ice quality variable
Belay placement may require pitons or slings; verify before moving rope
Second pitch transitions to mixed terrain; route-finding important
access_hazards: Eightmile Buttress area; approach via Icicle Road past Hubba Hubba area
Avalanche terrain near drainage; check NWAC forecast for slope stability
ice_quality: Mixed conditions; ice sections require good bonding to rock base

name: A Slice of Ice
watch_out: Limited documentation available; may be minor or lesser-visited flow
Ice quality and hazards not well-established in publicly available sources
Scout conditions on-site before committing to ascent
access_hazards: Icicle Creek area general hazards apply
Raptor closure Jan 1 - Aug 15 if near Bridge Creek Wall
seasonal_window: Likely December through March, similar to other Icicle flows
ice_quality: Unknown; likely water-ice formation on north-facing slope
